result: F-HOLO-5 (MIXED / Gate 1): holonomy-DFA (global entropy broadcast + local EXACT inverse-rotor transport) reaches 0.831 vs random-DFA 0.560 (chance) with grad-alignment +0.40 vs 0.00 -> exact rotor transport IS the lever (beats vanilla DFA). BUT depth not load-bearing under it (L3 0.831 ~= L1 0.815) and it trails sequential backprop 0.894 -> the global broadcast does NOT compose through depth (DFA ceiling). Gates SLAM. CONTINUATION F-HOLO-6: backward_dfa_transported (rotor-chain transport) RECOVERS depth-composition (L3 0.894 = exact backprop, Delta-depth +0.078 vs naive +0.016), align 0.41 (approximation not exact). = validated inter-shell transport primitive for concentric Gomb-Soma (design doc captured, build gated on Gate 2).

NEW_MECHANISM: F-HOLO-5: exact inverse-rotor feedback path lifts DFA off chance
NEGATIVE_RESULT_REQUIRING_A_GUARD: naive global broadcast does not compose through depth -> do not scale the naive rule to SLAM
novelty_classification.note: Answers the user's small-data gate: learning does NOT collapse, but the compositional-through-depth property (GAP #2) is absent from a naive broadcast.

components_added_or_updated: RotorMeshNet += backward_dfa / backward_from_rot_grads (alternative credit-assignment rules; sequential backward remains default)
guards_added: Gate-1 verdict logic in the example (holonomy-DFA must match sequential AND beat random to PASS; here PARTIAL)

gates.gate1: PARTIAL (holonomy-DFA beats random YES, matches sequential NO, uses depth NO)
gates.gate1_followup: F-HOLO-6 RESOLVES depth-composition: transported-DFA 0.894 = sequential, Delta-depth +0.078

integrity_note: Rule-vs-rule on the same net (valid); NOT a task-necessity claim (trivial beats the net here, F-HOLO-2, stated). The exact rotor transport being the lever is evidenced by both AUROC (0.831 vs 0.560) AND alignment (+0.40 vs 0.00), not one number.
honest_scope: Naive broadcast only; Adam only; trivial-solvable substrate. Depth-composing broadcast and a learning-necessary task (Gate 2) are the open rungs before SLAM.
next_experiment_authorized: Gate 2: a learning-necessary task (trivial AND fixed closed-form both fail) to test whether transported transport recovers depth where the net is NECESSARY; THEN the concentric Gomb-Soma build (docs/design-concentric-gomb-soma.md); THEN SLAM.
next_experiment_proposal: implement backward_dfa_transported (partial adjoint transport: each layer gets E transported by the product of inverse rotors from the top down to it, but no mesh re-mix between = between raw-broadcast and sequential); A/B vs the naive broadcast and sequential on the same substrate + alignment angle. If alignment rises toward 1 and L3>L1 returns, depth-composition is recovered.
